assessor_1_raw:
ASSESSOR 1 — real_world_framing_009

The CTR lift is real and the A/B test is well-designed. The deployment concern is not model quality — it is that the model optimizes for CTR without respect to contractual obligations. Third-party merchant agreements guarantee minimum impression shares; the new model's concentration (73% of impressions from 8% of catalog) likely breaches these guarantees for long-tail merchants. Additionally, 40% fewer impressions for low-ranked items suppresses product discovery and catalog health in ways a 2-week CTR test cannot detect. A merchant impression guarantee audit is required before full deployment, and diversity constraints on the ranking should be considered.
assessor_2_raw:
ASSESSOR 2 — real_world_framing_009

The A/B test validates CTR improvement. The critical pre-deployment requirement is a merchant impression guarantee compliance audit: the new model's concentration effect may breach contractual minimum impression share obligations. Long-tail suppression is a secondary but real catalog health concern with compounding effects. Both addressable by constrained ranking that enforces impression share minimums while maximizing CTR.
assessor_3_raw:
ASSESSOR 3 — real_world_framing_009

Two concerns: (1) contractual impression share guarantees for merchants may be breached — this is a legal and commercial risk that must be audited before rollout; (2) long-tail suppression has compound catalog health effects. Model quality is strong; the deployment requires pre-rollout compliance audit and consideration of impression share constraints.
synthesis_raw:
SYNTHESIS — real_world_framing_009 (Ensemble, Run 1)

All three assessors agree on both issues: merchant agreement impression guarantee compliance risk and catalog concentration/long-tail suppression. The model's CTR improvement is not disputed. The deployment requires a pre-deployment merchant impression guarantee audit and ongoing catalog health monitoring.

EMPIRICAL TEST SPECIFICATION: Audit all contracted merchants' expected impression shares under new model rankings before full deployment. Measure impression share for each contracted merchant vs. contractual minimums. If ≥5% of contracted merchants fall below guaranteed thresholds, constrained ranking is required before rollout. Monitor catalog breadth (items receiving >100 impressions/week) for 90 days post-deployment.

empirical_test.condition: Merchant impression guarantee compliance audit before deployment; 90-day catalog breadth monitoring post-deployment.
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: ≥5% of contracted merchants below guaranteed thresholds, or catalog breadth declines materially.
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Merchant compliance met; catalog breadth stable; CTR improvement holds.
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: 2–3 high-revenue merchants slightly below threshold requiring negotiation, but model otherwise compliant.
